commit.message:
perf: parallel LLM calls + Ollama JSON mode

Two major performance and reliability improvements:

1. asyncio.gather — all 8 chunk requests fire concurrently.
   Wall-clock time ≈ slowest single chunk instead of sum of all
   chunks (~8x faster on CPU, even more on GPU).

2. Ollama JSON mode (format="json") — Ollama constrains token
   sampling to always emit valid JSON. Eliminates code-fence
   stripping, regex fallbacks, and malformed-JSON retries.

commit.message:
Fix geocoding: clean concatenated addresses, use pin codes for precision

The Nominatim geocoder was returning city-center coordinates for most
cases because addresses were concatenated without spaces (e.g.,
"ChinchwadPuneMaharashtra411019"). This caused all cases to show the
same distance.

- Add address cleaning: split camelCase words, separate state/city names
- Extract Indian pin codes (6-digit) for more precise geocoding
- Multi-strategy geocoding: pin+area → pin only → cleaned address → location+area
- Re-geocode cases that got city-center coordinates
- Increase geocoding rounds from 3 to 15 for re-geocoding pass
- Re-fetch cases after geocoding completes to show updated distances

commit.message:
fix: use pdfjs-dist legacy build to resolve worker import error on Vercel

The PDF extraction API route was failing with:
"Cannot find module '/var/task/.next/server/app/api/extract/pdf.worker.mjs'"

Root cause: pdfjs-dist v4 uses an ESM worker (pdf.worker.min.mjs) that
isn't bundled by Next.js standalone output for the Vercel serverless
environment. The next.config.js externals config also referenced the
wrong file extension (.js instead of .mjs).

Fix: Switch to pdfjs-dist/legacy/build/pdf.mjs which processes PDFs on
the main thread without requiring a separate worker module, and remove
the now-unnecessary worker externals config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate 20s+ clipboard latency via two-stage transcription

Root causes:
- _transcription_lock serialised all recordings — second recording waited
  for first transcription (Whisper + LLM) to fully complete before starting
- clipboard was updated only after BOTH Whisper + LLM finished; no early
  feedback to user

Fix: split pipeline into two independent stages
- Stage 1 (~3-5s): Whisper only → paste/copy immediately so user has text
- Stage 2 (non-blocking): LLM correction → silent clipboard-only update,
  no re-paste; user can manually Ctrl+V to get corrected version
- Remove _transcription_lock entirely; recordings now process concurrently
- Add TranscriptionService.correct() as public entry point for Stage 2

* refactor(rag): remove plugin_adapter, inline logic into RuntimeKnowledgeBase

BREAKING CHANGE: RAGPluginAdapter has been removed. All plugin
communication is now handled directly by RuntimeKnowledgeBase.

Architecture change:
- Before: RuntimeKnowledgeBase → RAGPluginAdapter → plugin_connector
- After:  RuntimeKnowledgeBase → plugin_connector (direct)

Changes to kbmgr.py (RuntimeKnowledgeBase):
- Remove RAGPluginAdapter import and usage
- Inline plugin communication methods:
  - _on_kb_create(): Notify plugin when KB is created
  - _on_kb_delete(): Notify plugin when KB is deleted
  - _ingest_document(): Call plugin for document ingestion
  - _retrieve(): Call plugin for retrieval
  - _delete_document(): Call plugin to delete document
- Simplify dispose(): Only notify plugin, no built-in VDB assumption

Changes to base.py (KnowledgeBaseInterface):
- Remove get_type() abstract method (outdated internal/external concept)
- Add get_rag_engine_plugin_id() abstract method

Changes to localagent.py:
- Remove get_type() call
- Simplify top_k retrieval from KB entity

Deleted files:

Benefits:
- Reduced abstraction layer, simpler code
- Plugin communication logic centralized in RuntimeKnowledgeBase
- Easier to understand and maintain

(doubled quote). The backslash escape is not recognized by OceanBase
in NO_BACKSLASH_ESCAPES mode, causing SQL syntax errors when metadata
text contains apostrophes (e.g. O'Shea in academic citations).
